title: potentilla laeta var. micrantha
intro: Potentilla laeta var. micrantha, also known as dwarf cinquefoil, is a shrubby perennial plant belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been attributed to its flavonoid and phenolic content. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are typically harvested and dried to make infusions, tinctures, or teas, which are used to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
